steps.title: Find where the sophist reveals his exaggerated sense
steps.content: We need a quotation where a sophist directly shows — through his own words or expectations — that he overestimates his own wisdom.

steps.title: Identify the sophist's self-assessment
steps.content: Choice D has Socrates expecting students to "seize immediately" his "clever dogmas" — his own words reveal how highly he regards his wisdom and how easily he assumes others will grasp it. This directly shows exaggerated self-assessment.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the passage shows a sophist dismissing an opponent's view. Dismissal of others is not the same as overestimating one's own wisdom; it does not reveal how highly the sophist rates himself. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — the passage describes what sophists teach or the content of their lessons. Describing a teaching topic says nothing about whether the sophist thinks too highly of his own expertise. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — the passage shows a sophist encouraging a student. Encouragement reflects the sophist's attitude toward the student, not an inflated assessment of his own wisdom. Eliminated.
Choice D is the only quotation where the sophist's own words betray an exaggerated view of his wisdom — D is correct.
